diagrams.title: Spiral Model
diagrams.description: Shows iterative cycles expanding outward with four phases in each spiral: Planning, Risk Analysis, Engineering, and Evaluation
elements: Risk analysis decreases as spiral progresses
diagrams.purpose: Understanding risk-driven iterative development and how each cycle produces a more complete version

diagrams.title: V-Model Structure
diagrams.description: V-shaped diagram mapping development phases on left to testing phases on right
elements: Bottom right to top right: Unit → Integration → System → Acceptance Testing
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing one-to-one correspondence between development and testing phases for quality assurance

diagrams.title: Incremental Model
diagrams.description: Shows sequential increments building upon each other from baseline
elements: Customer receives usable product after each increment
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how functional modules are delivered progressively to customers

diagrams.title: Process Model Comparison
diagrams.description: Comparison chart showing Waterfall, Incremental, Iterative, and Spiral characteristics
elements: Waterfall: Sequential, high risk, late testing
Shows flexibility, risk, time, complexity for each
diagrams.purpose: Choosing appropriate model based on project requirements and constraints

visuals.title: Specialized Process Models
visuals.description: Visual diagram for specialized process models
